questions.subject: NSEP
questions.q: A particle moves in XY plane according to the relations $x=k t$ and $y=k t(1-p t)$ where $k$ and $p$ are positive constants and $t$ is time. Therefore,
options: the trajectory of the particle is a parabola.
the particle has a constant velocity along X axis.
the force acting on the particle remains in the same direction even if both $k$ and $p$ are negative constants.
the particle has a constant acceleration along -Y axis.

questions.subject: NSEP
questions.q:
A particle starting from rest at the highest point slides down the outside of a smooth vertical circular track of radius 0.3 m . When it leaves the track its vertical fall is $h$ and the linear velocity is $v$. The angle made by the radius at that position of the particle with the vertical is $\theta$. Now consider the following observations: $\left(g=10 \mathrm{~m} / \mathrm{s}^2\right)$
(I) $h=0.1 \mathrm{~m}$ and $\cos \theta=\frac{2}{3}$. (II) $h=0.2 \mathrm{~m}$ and $\cos \theta=1 / 3$. (III) $v=\sqrt{2} \mathrm{~ms}^{-1}$. (IV) After leaving the circular track the particle will describe a parabolic path.

Therefore,
